channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rashida Tlaib becomes lone House lawmaker opposing cracking down on Mexican cartels' border tunnel system
item.description: The House of Representatives voted on a bill to help Congress crack down on Mexican drug cartels' use of tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS expedites process to award two Republican-linked firms part of its $200 million ad campaign
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security skipped a fully competitive bidding process to give two Republican-linked firms the first part of a $200 million anti-immigration ad campaign.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge blocks deportation of Columbia University protester
item.description: A Palestinian green card holder, Khalil earned his master's degree in December, and his wife, a U.S. citizen, is eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pushes detente between Musk and Rubio
item.description: Elon Musk and Secretary of State Marco Rubio sought to project unity Monday, following a private clash at a recent Cabinet meeting, with President Trump seeking to play mediator.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration needs to be 'shut down' until unity restored: Carlson
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged history's largest domestic deportation, but some fear how long the process could take
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Dow plunges 890 points after Trump declines to rule out recession
item.description: Stocks have fallen steadily since the start of March in response to a string of underwhelming economic data and whipsaw tariff announcements from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: GOP lawmaker takes victory lap after Dem mayor reportedly agrees to donate 'secret' gifts to charities
item.description: Rep. Brandon Gill, R-Texas, is celebrating after Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson agreed to donate gifts that the city received to charity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz reveals the missteps he saw with Harris campaign amid postmortem media blitz
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, the failed 2024 Democratic vice presidential candidate, revealed the missteps he saw with the Harris presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued President Donald Trump's Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing a legally awarded grant.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump loves the Gilded Age and its tariffs. It was a great time for the rich but not for the many
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In President Donald Trump's idealized framing, the United States was at its zenith in the 1890s, when top hats and shirtwaists were fashionable and typhoid fever often killed more soldiers than combat.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans. The motion for emergency relief was filed late Friday in federal court in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Singing Into Irrelevancy
item.description: Gump logic: Forrest Gump’s mama was right – stupid is as stupid does. ICYMI: For those that don't know, ICYMI is how the cool kids say “In case you missed it.” Sibling shoutout: The smarter and more talented Vittert child – aka my sister, Liberty – is out with a must-read in The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmaker proposes searchable domestic violence offender database
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would create a searchable database to see if someone has multiple domestic violence-related convictions to their name.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Targeted Tesla attacks continue amid outrage at Elon Musk
item.description: Some Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and charging stations have been set on fire or vandalized in other ways, ostensibly in protest of Musk's role.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New fines and awareness help reduce litter on Minnesota lakes, but advocates want more action
item.description: Lake advocates say a recent state law is helping curb the problem of trash left on Minnesota’s frozen lakes. But they're hoping for more action, including passage of a bill requiring state agencies establish a statewide “Keep It Clean” program and provide funding for local cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Following Trump’s lead, Gov. Abbott pushes state agencies to end telework
item.description: The directive comes as some state agencies have downsized their office spaces after the pandemic forced many employees to work remotely.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
